description: Lightweight and Pythonic API for parsing and creating XML data. Provides Element, ElementTree, and iterparse for efficient XML processing.

functions.name: parse

params.name: source
params.description: Filename or file-like object containing XML data

params.name: parser
params.description: Optional XML parser instance. If not given, the standard XMLParser is used.

returns.description: An ElementTree instance with the parsed document

functions.description: Parse an XML document from a file into an ElementTree. The source can be a filename string or a file-like object.

functions.name: fromstring

returns.description: The root Element of the parsed XML

functions.description: Parse an XML string and return the root Element. Equivalent to XML().

functions.name: tostring

params.name: encoding
params.description: Output encoding. Use 'unicode' to get a Unicode string instead of bytes.

returns.description: XML string representation of the element and its children

functions.description: Generate a string representation of an XML element, including all subelements.

functions.name: Element

params.name: **extra
params.description: Additional attributes given as keyword arguments

functions.description: Create a new Element with the given tag and attributes. This is the Element factory function.

functions.name: SubElement

params.name: parent
params.description: The parent element to append the new element to

params.name: tag
params.description: The tag name for the new subelement

params.name: **extra
params.description: Additional attributes given as keyword arguments

functions.description: Create a new Element and append it to the parent element. This is a convenience function combining Element creation and appending.

functions.name: Comment

returns.description: An Element representing an XML comment

functions.description: Create a comment element. The comment element has a special tag and the given text.

returns.description: An Element representing an XML processing instruction

functions.name: XML

returns.description: The root Element of the parsed XML

functions.description: Parse an XML string and return the root Element. Same as fromstring().

functions.name: XMLID

returns.description: A tuple of (root Element, dict mapping id attributes to Elements)

functions.description: Parse an XML string and return a tuple of the root element and a dictionary mapping id attributes to their elements.

functions.name: register_namespace
functions.description: Register a namespace prefix. The prefix is used in serialization to generate namespace declarations. The registry is global.

functions.name: iselement

returns.description: True if the object appears to be an Element

functions.description: Check if an object appears to be a valid Element object. Tests for tag attribute presence.

functions.name: dump

returns.description: Writes to sys.stdout; no return value

functions.description: Write an element tree or element structure to sys.stdout as XML. Useful for debugging.

functions.name: iterparse

params.name: source
params.description: Filename or file-like object containing XML data

params.name: events
params.description: Sequence of events to report. Supported: 'start', 'end', 'start-ns', 'end-ns'. Default is ('end',).

functions.description: Incrementally parse an XML document and yield (event, element) tuples. Memory-efficient for large documents since elements can be discarded after processing.

classes.name: Element
classes.description: The Element type is a flexible container object for storing hierarchical data. Each element has a tag, text, tail, a dictionary of attributes, and a list of child elements.

constructors.description: Create a new Element with the given tag and attributes.

methods.name: append

params.name: subelement
params.description: Element to add as a child

methods.description: Add a subelement to the end of this element's list of children.

methods.name: extend

params.name: subelements
params.description: Iterable of Element objects to add

methods.description: Append subelements from an iterable of elements.

methods.name: find

params.name: match
params.description: XPath-like path expression to match. Supports tag names, '.', '*', '//', '..', and [@attrib] predicates.

returns.description: First matching Element or None if not found

methods.description: Find the first subelement matching the given path expression. Returns the first match or None.

methods.name: findall
methods.description: Find all matching subelements. Returns a list of all matching elements in document order.

methods.name: findtext

params.name: default
params.description: Value to return if no match is found

returns.description: Text content of the first matching element, or default

methods.description: Find text for the first subelement matching the path. Returns the text content or the default value.

methods.name: getiterator

params.name: tag
params.description: Tag to filter by. If None or '*', all elements are included.

returns.description: List of elements in document order

methods.description: Return a list of all elements in the subtree in document order. Deprecated; use iter() instead.

methods.name: iter

params.name: tag
params.description: Tag to filter by. If None or '*', all elements are yielded.

returns.description: Iterator over matching Element objects in document order

methods.description: Create a tree iterator over this element and all subelements. Yields elements in document order.

methods.name: iterfind
methods.description: Find all matching subelements by path expression. Returns an iterable yielding matched elements in document order.

methods.name: itertext
methods.description: Create a text iterator. Yields all inner text from this element and its subelements, in document order.

methods.name: insert
methods.description: Insert a subelement at the given position.

methods.name: items

returns.description: List of (name, value) tuples for element attributes

methods.description: Return element attributes as a list of (name, value) pairs.

methods.name: keys
methods.description: Return a list of element attribute names.

methods.name: get

params.name: default
params.description: Value to return if attribute is not found

methods.description: Get an element attribute value. Returns the default if the attribute is not found.

methods.name: set
methods.description: Set an element attribute value.

methods.name: remove
methods.description: Remove a subelement from the element. Compares by identity, not value. Raises ValueError if the subelement is not found.

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Reset the element. Removes all subelements, clears all attributes, and sets text and tail to None.

methods.name: makeelement

params.name: tag
params.description: Tag name for the new element

params.name: attrib
params.description: Attribute dictionary for the new element

methods.description: Create a new element with the same type as this element. Subclasses may override to control element instantiation.

fields.name: text
fields.description: Text content before the first child element, or None

fields.name: tail
fields.description: Text content after this element's end tag and before the next sibling, or None

classes.name: ElementTree
classes.description: Represents an entire XML document as a tree. Wraps a root Element and provides methods for serialization and parsing.

params.name: element
params.description: Root element to use. If not given and file is provided, the root is loaded from the file.

params.name: file
params.description: Filename or file-like object to load XML from

constructors.description: Create a new ElementTree. If element is given, it becomes the root. If file is given, the tree is initialized from the file.

methods.name: parse

params.name: source
params.description: Filename or file-like object to parse

returns.description: The root element of the parsed tree

methods.description: Load an external XML document into this ElementTree. Replaces the current root element. Returns the new root.

methods.name: getroot

returns.description: The root Element of the tree

methods.description: Return the root element of this tree.

methods.name: write

params.name: file
params.description: Filename or file-like object to write to

params.name: xml_declaration
params.description: Whether to write an XML declaration. None means write it only if encoding is not us-ascii or UTF-8.

returns.description: Writes to file; no return value

methods.description: Write the element tree to a file as XML.

methods.name: find
methods.description: Find the first element matching the path, starting from the root.

methods.name: findall
methods.description: Find all elements matching the path, starting from the root.

methods.name: findtext

params.name: default
params.description: Value to return if no match

returns.description: Text content of the first match, or default

methods.description: Find text for the first element matching the path, starting from the root.

methods.name: iter

params.name: tag
params.description: Tag to filter by. If None or '*', all elements are yielded.

methods.description: Create and return a tree iterator for the root element and all subelements.

methods.name: iterfind
methods.description: Find all matching elements by path. Returns an iterable yielding matched elements.

classes.name: XMLParser
classes.description: An incremental XML parser driven by explicit push calls. Builds an element structure from XML text incrementally.

params.name: html
params.description: Set to non-zero to use HTML parsing mode (deprecated and not well supported)

params.name: target
params.description: Target object with start(), end(), data(), and close() methods. Defaults to TreeBuilder.

params.name: encoding
params.description: Optional encoding to override the encoding declared in the XML

constructors.description: Create an XML parser with optional target and encoding.

methods.name: feed

params.name: data
params.description: XML text chunk to feed to the parser

methods.description: Feed XML data to the parser. Can be called multiple times with chunks of data.

methods.name: close

returns.description: The root element of the parsed tree

methods.description: Finish feeding data to the parser. Returns the result of calling the target's close() method, which is typically the root element.
